game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Ultimate Custom Night**: Players are tasked with surviving a single night, typically lasting from 12 AM to 6 AM, within a fixed-view office by strategically managing limited power and various defensive systems against up to 50 selectable animatronic characters. Gameplay involves tapping on UI elements to close two side doors, two vents, and two air hoses, activate a power generator, heater, A/C, and a global music box, while also monitoring two Pirate Cove curtains, all consuming a finite power resource. Each animatronic, with customizable difficulty from 0-20, possesses unique behaviors and entry points, requiring players to react to audio cues and visual alerts to prevent jumpscares. Players can also deploy laser traps in vents, collect Faz-Coins from specific animatronic interactions, and purchase temporary defensive items from a Prize Counter, with the primary win condition being to survive until 6 AM. Progression is achieved by completing 16 themed challenges, which unlock new office skins and cutscenes, while a jumpscare from any animatronic results in immediate failure.
game_info.description: Survive a single night from 12 AM to 6 AM in your office while managing power and defending against multiple animatronic characters. Each animatronic has unique behaviors and attack patterns. Use doors, vents, and various systems to prevent jumpscares while monitoring your limited power supply. Collect Faz-Coins to purchase defensive items and survive until 6 AM to win!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate menu selections and UI elements
Space: Activate/toggle selected system (doors, vents, etc.)
Z: Quick-use emergency power boost
Shift: Open Prize Counter shop
Enter: Start game from menu
Esc: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart and return to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic game mechanics including power management, door controls, and survival for a short duration. This validates that the core defensive systems work correctly and power consumption is functioning.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Navigate through UI elements using arrow keys, activate doors and vents strategically to conserve power. Monitor animatronic positions and close appropriate defenses when threats are detected. Use power boost sparingly to extend survival time.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Successfully survive for at least 2 in-game hours (about 30-40 seconds real time) without being jumpscared. Power should decrease gradually, and defensive systems should successfully block animatronic attacks. Test passes if gameState shows continued survival.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing the full win condition by surviving until 6 AM. This validates the complete game loop, all animatronic behaviors, power management strategy, and victory state transition.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Implement optimal power conservation strategy by only using defenses when absolutely necessary. Prioritize high-threat animatronics, use the power generator at critical moments, and collect Faz-Coins when safe. Balance all defensive systems to reach 6 AM with remaining power.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully reach 6 AM and trigger GAME_OVER_WIN state. The test is successful when gameState.gamePhase transitions to "GAME_OVER_WIN" and the victory screen is displayed with appropriate message.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing animatronic threat detection and response systems. Validates that the game correctly identifies when animatronics are at attack positions and that appropriate defensive actions prevent jumpscares.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Monitor all animatronic positions through gameState, react immediately when any animatronic reaches a critical position. Test each defense type (doors, vents, hoses) against their corresponding threats. Verify visual and logical feedback matches threat levels.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: All animatronic attacks should be successfully blocked when appropriate defenses are active. No jumpscare should occur when defenses are properly deployed. Test validates collision detection and defense mechanics work correctly.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing the Prize Counter and Faz-Coin economy. Validates that coins can be collected from specific interactions and spent on defensive items that provide tangible benefits.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Intentionally trigger Faz-Coin earning conditions by interacting with specific animatronics. Open Prize Counter with Shift key, purchase various items, and verify they provide the expected defensive benefits during gameplay.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Faz-Coins should accumulate correctly, Prize Counter UI should display available items, purchases should deduct coins and grant items, and items should provide measurable defensive advantages. Test passes when item effects are confirmed.
